title: Hooke's Law in Everyday Life
script_summary: Exploring the surprising limits of Hooke's Law through everyday examples.

segments.voiceover: Many springs stretch proportional to force—until they don’t.
Hooke's Law states that the force needed to stretch a spring is proportional to its extension.
But what happens when you push too hard? The spring can break!
Everyday items, like a trampoline, show Hooke's Law in action. Bounce too hard, and it might snap.
Understanding these limits can make you safer in sports or engineering.
Curious about more physics surprises? Like and follow for more!
